title: Carl Linnaeus
ground_truth_corrupted_sentence: Even in his early years, Linnaeus seemed to have a liking for plants, herbs in particular.
guessed_corrupted_sentence: Linnaeus is designated as the type specimen for the human species, Homo sapiens.

parsed_response.corrupted_sentence: Linnaeus is designated as the type specimen for the human species, Homo sapiens.
parsed_response.explanation: This is a common misconception. While Homo sapiens Linnaeus, 1758 is the type species of the genus Homo, no person—certainly not Linnaeus himself—has been designated as the name-bearing type (holotype/lectotype/neotype) for the species. The ICZN does not recognize a living individual as a valid type specimen, and no ruling designates Linnaeus as such. Linnaeus described the species without a holotype, and later nomenclatural actions have not made him the type specimen.
